L1.area: Bytecode compilation backend and runtime memory layout management

L2.description: A buffer overflow vulnerability exists in a bytecode compiler where memory allocation for nested composite-type arrays incorrectly uses the logical element count instead of accounting for the actual memory footprint of each element. When composite elements such as tuples occupy multiple memory slots, the allocated buffer is too small, and subsequent writes to fill the array corrupt adjacent heap memory.

L3.area: Brillig bytecode compiler's memory pre-allocation for foreign call return values
L3.description: A buffer overflow vulnerability exists in the Brillig bytecode compiler where pre-allocation of heap memory for nested arrays returned by foreign function calls incorrectly uses the semantic element count rather than the semi-flattened size that accounts for composite element types like tuples. This causes under-allocation when each array element occupies more than one memory slot, leading to heap corruption when the virtual machine writes the returned data.

ground_truth.reason: The allocate_foreign_call_result_array function uses only the semantic element count when allocating heap memory for nested arrays of composite types, discarding the inner type information. This under-allocates the buffer, causing heap corruption when the VM writes data for elements that each occupy multiple memory slots.
